---
title: "G1-2 synthetic output"
subtitle: "recursive training essai"
date: "2026-05-25"
categories:
   - LLMs
   - model collapse
bibliography: LXtech.bib
format: html
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
toc-expand: true

# css: https://ada-sub.dh-index.org/school/css/style.css
---

-----

> [litKI](lki-index.qmd) / [bajohr](lki-002.qmd) / model collapse

-----

# play along model collapse
## Qs
1. (script) > @schwarz_model_2026
2. (corpus) > @glaser_benjaminfeldkraft_2024

## method
the model (untrained, blank) has been trained (G1) on above corpus with above linked script (gpt proposal using torch in [R]) and retrained (G2) on the same corpus plus 10% synthetic output of G1. 

generally the training data (200.000 words) is not enough to output serious content even in G1, but we see literally that the model degenerates quickly; we observe entropy drop and decreasing vocabulary (89 vs. 86 char types).

## output G1, M3
{{< include synthetic_gen_3-1.md >}}

## output G2, M3
{{< include synthetic_gen_3-2.md >}}

## output G1, M7
showing another output of G1, this time the model has been trained on token=word level, which results in far more readable, nevertheless crumbled text... / the G2 ff. of that model is still to test after script adaptation.

{{< include synthetic_gen_7c-1.md >}}

# references
